title: tetracera alnifolia subsp. dinklagei
intro: Tetracera alnifolia subsp. dinklagei, also known as the West African woody vine, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which have been reported to aid in the treatment of various health issues. The health benefits of Tetracera alnifolia subsp. dinklagei include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the immune system, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
